title: Cosmos Whack — bop glowing creatures before they duck back down
summary: A real, self-contained whack-a-mole game in one HTML file with zero dependencies. Glowing Cosmos creatures pop up from a 3x3 grid of holes; click or tap them (or use the Q/W/E A/S/D Z/X/C keys mapped to the nine holes) to whack them for points before they duck back down, while a 30-second timer counts down and a miss counter tracks the ones you let escape. A hit splash and +10 popup confirm every whack; press R or the button to restart. It ships with a deterministic self-playing attract demo for capture: a seeded pop schedule and a scripted auto-player whack every mole on cue.
usage_notes: RUN: double-click index.html. CONTROLS: click/tap a glowing mole, or press keys Q W E / A S D / Z X C for the top, middle and bottom rows of the 3x3 grid; mis-clicks count as a miss. GOAL: score 10 points per whacked mole inside the 30-second round; the HUD shows SCORE, TIME and MISS; press R or the Restart button to play again. KEY TECHNIQUES: the whole round is a deterministic simulation — mulberry32 seeds a fixed pop schedule (hole, color, up-duration) and a scripted auto-player times each whack; AUTO is a pure function of time, drawAt(ms) re-derives score, the mole currently up and any active splash from ms alone, so window.__seek(t) is reproducible. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 self-plays + window.__seek(t). TUNE: ROUND length, POP_MS up-time, LOOP, T0OFF (frame-0 offset), grid via COLS/ROWS, mole colors MOLECOL.
